loungeAccess.conditions: Domestic: 4 visits per quarter, gated on ₹75,000 spend in the previous calendar quarter (unlocks access for the following quarter). International: a total of 2 complimentary visits per calendar year (not per quarter), with no minimum spend requirement, primary cardholder only.

movieBenefit.description: Complimentary movie/event ticket offer via BookMyShow
movieBenefit.frequency: Requires ₹25,000+ spend in the preceding calendar quarter

milestoneBenefits.reward: Annual fee waiver plus 48 complimentary golf games

source.name: ICICI Bank
source.document: Upcoming changes to Credit Card features and charges

faqTemplateAnswers.Is this card lifetime free?: No. It carries a joining fee of ₹6,500 + GST and an annual fee of ₹3,500 + GST from the second year, waived if you spend over ₹6 lakh in the preceding year.
faqTemplateAnswers.Is it worth it?: It can be worth it for frequent domestic/international travellers who can clear the ₹75,000/quarter spend needed for lounge access and reach ₹6 lakh in annual spend for the fee waiver and golf benefits; casual spenders may find the fees hard to justify.
faqTemplateAnswers.Who should get this card?: Frequent flyers and travel-focused spenders who can consistently spend ₹75,000+ per quarter to unlock lounge access, and who value air accident cover and BookMyShow movie offers.
faqTemplateAnswers.Does it support UPI?: The RuPay variant of the Sapphiro can be linked to UPI apps; the standard Visa/Mastercard variants cannot be used for UPI payments.
faqTemplateAnswers.Can I get lounge access with this card?: Yes — 4 complimentary domestic lounge visits per quarter (conditional on ₹75,000 spend in the prior quarter) and 2 complimentary international lounge visits per calendar year, for the primary cardholder.
faqTemplateAnswers.What income is required to qualify?: ICICI Bank does not publish a fixed minimum income for the Sapphiro, but as a premium travel card it typically requires a stable, high income/salary and a strong credit history.
faqTemplateAnswers.Who should avoid this card?: Low or irregular spenders who won't reach the ₹75,000 quarterly spend for lounge access, since the fees are relatively high for a card whose best perks are spend-gated.
faqTemplateAnswers.What are the best alternatives?: HDFC Regalia Gold or Axis Magnus for higher travel value at a similar or higher fee, or a lifetime-free card like Kotak 811 if the annual fee itself is the concern.
